title: Claude Code Hook Events
description: Canonical IR for Claude Code hook event I/O shapes. Reference-authored per ADR 0006. CC version 2.1.206.

OpenObject.description: Open JSON object — tool_input and tool_response left untyped per ADR 0006.

cwd.description: Current working directory when the hook is invoked.

hook_event_name.description: Name of the event that fired.

permission_mode.description: Current permission mode. Not all events include this field.

effort.description: Active effort level for the turn. Present for events that fire within a tool-use context when the current model supports the effort parameter.

agent_id.description: Unique identifier for the subagent. Present only when the hook fires inside a subagent call.

agent_type.description: Agent name. Present when the session uses --agent or the hook fires inside a subagent.

prompt_id.description: UUID identifying the user prompt (turn) currently being processed. Matches the prompt.id attribute on OpenTelemetry events, so hook output can be correlated with telemetry for a single prompt. Absent until the first user input (e.g. session lifecycle events before any prompt). Requires Claude Code v2.1.196 or later.

continue.description: If false, Claude stops processing entirely after the hook runs.

stopReason.description: Message shown to the user when continue is false. Not shown to Claude.

suppressOutput.description: If true, hides the hook's stdout from the transcript.

systemMessage.description: Warning message shown to the user.

terminalSequence.description: One or more terminal escape sequences for Claude Code to emit through its own terminal write path (race-free, works inside tmux/screen and on Windows). Each sequence must be terminated with BEL (\u0007) or ST (ESC \\). Multiple sequences may be concatenated into a single string. Allowed: OSC 0/1/2 (window and icon title), OSC 9 (iTerm2, ConEmu, WezTerm, Windows Terminal notifications; OSC 9;4 sets taskbar progress), OSC 99 (Kitty notifications), OSC 777 (urxvt, Ghostty, Warp notifications), bare BEL. Anything outside this allowlist — CSI cursor/color sequences, OSC 8 hyperlinks, OSC 52 clipboard writes, OSC 1337, OSC palette sequences — is rejected and the entire field is ignored.

type.description: Friendly task-type label such as shell, subagent, monitor, workflow, teammate, cloud session, or MCP task.

command.description: Shell command line. Present only for shell tasks.

agent_type.description: Subagent type name. Present only for subagent tasks.

server.description: MCP server name. Present only for monitor and MCP task tasks.

tool.description: MCP tool name. Present only for monitor and MCP task tasks.

name.description: Workflow name. Present only for workflow tasks.

recurring.description: false for one-shot wakeups, true for tasks that re-fire on every match.

prompt.description: Prompt submitted when the cron fires, capped at 1000 characters.

tool_response.description: The serialized tool_result content the model sees. A string or content-block array.

PermissionSuggestion.description: An always-allow option from the permission dialog. Same shape as PermissionUpdateEntry.

model.description: Model identifier. Only SessionStart hooks receive this field. Absent on the --init-only launch path.

session_title.description: Current session title if one is already set.

trigger.description: CLI flag that triggered the hook.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the instruction file that was loaded.

globs.description: Path glob patterns from the file's paths: frontmatter. Present only for path_glob_match loads.

trigger_file_path.description: Path to the file whose access triggered this load, for lazy loads.

parent_file_path.description: Path to the parent instruction file that included this one, for include loads.

expansion_type.description: slash_command for skill and custom commands, mcp_prompt for MCP server prompts.

command_name.description: The command name that was expanded.

message_id.description: UUID of the assistant message being displayed. Stable across every batch of the same message.

index.description: Zero-based index of this batch within the message.

final.description: true on the message's last batch. Each message has exactly one final batch.

delta.description: The newly completed lines since the prior batch. Always whole lines except the final batch.

tool_name.description: Name of the tool being called.

tool_input.description: Tool input parameters. Shape depends on the tool.

tool_use_id.description: Unique identifier for this tool call.

tool_name.description: Name of the tool requesting permission.

tool_input.description: Tool input parameters. Shape depends on the tool.

permission_suggestions.description: The always-allow options the user would normally see in the permission dialog.

tool_name.description: Name of the tool that completed.

tool_response.description: Result returned by the tool. Object for built-in tools; content-block array for MCP tools.

tool_use_id.description: Unique identifier for this tool call.

duration_ms.description: Tool execution time in milliseconds. Excludes time in permission prompts and PreToolUse hooks.

tool_name.description: Name of the tool that failed.

tool_use_id.description: Unique identifier for this tool call.

is_interrupt.description: Whether the failure was caused by user interruption.

is_timeout.description: Whether the failure was a timeout.

tool_calls.description: Every tool call in the batch.

tool_name.description: Name of the tool that was denied.

tool_use_id.description: Unique identifier for the denied tool call.

reason.description: The classifier's explanation for why the tool call was denied.

agent_type.description: Agent name (built-in or custom agent name from frontmatter).

stop_hook_active.description: true when Claude Code is already continuing as a result of a stop hook.

agent_type.description: Agent name used for matcher filtering.

agent_transcript_path.description: Path to the subagent's own transcript stored in a nested subagents/ folder.

last_assistant_message.description: Text content of the subagent's final response.

background_tasks.description: In-flight background tasks scoped to the parent session.

session_crons.description: Session-scoped scheduled wakeups scoped to the parent session.

task_id.description: Identifier of the task being created.

teammate_name.description: Name of the teammate creating the task.

task_id.description: Identifier of the task being completed.

teammate_name.description: Name of the teammate completing the task.

stop_hook_active.description: true when Claude Code is already continuing as a result of a stop hook. Claude Code overrides the hook and ends the turn after 8 consecutive blocks.

last_assistant_message.description: Text content of Claude's final response.

error_details.description: Additional details about the error, when available.

last_assistant_message.description: The rendered error text shown in the conversation.

teammate_name.description: Name of the teammate that is about to go idle.

file_path.description: Path to the specific file that was modified.
Absolute path to the file that changed.

name.description: Slug identifier for the new worktree, either user-specified or auto-generated.

worktree_path.description: Absolute path to the worktree being removed.

trigger.description: Whether compaction was triggered manually (/compact) or automatically.

custom_instructions.description: For manual: what the user passed into /compact, or null when none was passed. For auto: empty string or null.

trigger.description: Whether compaction was manual or automatic.

compact_summary.description: The conversation summary generated by the compact operation.

mcp_server_name.description: Name of the MCP server requesting input.

message.description: The prompt message from the MCP server.

mode.description: form for form-based elicitation, url for browser-based authentication.

url.description: Authentication URL. Present for url-mode elicitation.

elicitation_id.description: Unique identifier for this elicitation request.

requested_schema.description: JSON Schema object describing the form fields. Present for form-mode elicitation.

content.description: Form field values submitted by the user. Present when action is accept.

additionalContext.description: String added to Claude's context at the start of the conversation.

initialUserMessage.description: String used as the first user message of the session. Applies in non-interactive mode (-p).

sessionTitle.description: Sets the session title. Applies only when source is startup or resume.

watchPaths.description: Array of absolute paths to watch for FileChanged events during this session.

reloadSkills.description: When true, Claude Code re-scans the skill and command directories after SessionStart hooks complete.

decision.description: block prevents the prompt from being processed. Omit to allow.

reason.description: Shown to the user when decision is block.

suppressOriginalPrompt.description: If true when decision is block, omits the original prompt text from the block message.

additionalContext.description: String added to Claude's context alongside the submitted prompt.

sessionTitle.description: Sets the session title based on prompt content.

decision.description: block prevents the slash command from expanding. Omit to allow.

reason.description: Shown to the user when decision is block.

additionalContext.description: String added to Claude's context alongside the expanded prompt.

displayContent.description: Text displayed in place of the delta. Omit to display the original.

permissionDecision.description: allow skips the permission prompt. deny prevents the tool call. ask prompts the user. defer exits gracefully for later resumption.

permissionDecisionReason.description: For allow and ask: shown to the user. For deny: shown to Claude.

updatedInput.description: Modifies the tool's input parameters before execution. Replaces the entire input object.

additionalContext.description: String added to Claude's context alongside the tool result.

behavior.description: allow grants the permission, deny denies it.

updatedInput.description: For allow only: modifies the tool's input parameters before execution.

updatedPermissions.description: For allow only: permission update entries to apply.

message.description: For deny only: tells Claude why the permission was denied.

interrupt.description: For deny only: if true, stops Claude.

decision.description: block adds the reason next to the tool result. Claude still sees the original output.

reason.description: Explanation shown to Claude when decision is block.

additionalContext.description: String added to Claude's context alongside the tool result.

updatedToolOutput.description: Replaces the tool's output with the provided value before it is sent to Claude. Must match the tool's output shape.

updatedMCPToolOutput.description: Replaces the output for MCP tools only. Prefer updatedToolOutput.

decision.description: block value per the decision control summary table.

additionalContext.description: String added to Claude's context alongside the error.

decision.description: block stops the agentic loop before the next model call.

additionalContext.description: Context string injected once before the next model call.

retry.description: When true, Claude Code tells the model it may retry the denied tool call.

additionalContext.description: String added to the subagent's context at the start of its conversation.

decision.description: block keeps the subagent running and delivers reason to the subagent as its next instruction.

additionalContext.description: Non-error feedback for the subagent. Conversation continues without a hook error notification.

reason.description: Required when decision is block. Tells Claude why it should continue.

additionalContext.description: Non-error feedback for Claude. Conversation continues, transcript labels it as Stop hook feedback.

decision.description: block prevents the configuration change from being applied. Note: policy_settings changes cannot be blocked.

reason.description: Explanation shown to the user when decision is block.

watchPaths.description: Array of absolute paths to register with the FileChanged watcher (replaces the current dynamic watch list). Behaviorally verified: a CwdChanged hook returning hookSpecificOutput.watchPaths fires FileChanged when one of those paths is modified.

Array of absolute paths to dynamically update the watch list. Behaviorally verified: a FileChanged hook returning hookSpecificOutput.watchPaths fires FileChanged when one of those newly-registered paths is modified.

worktreePath.description: Absolute path where the worktree should be created. Required — Claude Code fails the creation if the path is missing. run() prints this to stdout for command hooks; HTTP hooks return it in the JSON response.

decision.description: block prevents compaction. For manual /compact the stderr message is shown to the user.

action.description: Whether to accept, decline, or cancel the request.

content.description: Form field values to submit. Only used when action is accept.
Overrides form field values. Only meaningful when action is accept.
